notes: UC Santa Cruz students may complete one or more minors, provided they can finish all required coursework within their maximum enrollment terms and credit limits. Minors are recorded on the official transcript but do not appear on the diploma. Pursuing a minor is optional and should align with a student’s academic interests and career goals. Students do not apply for minors as part of their UC Santa Cruz admission application. Transfer students interested in a minor should reach out to the department that offers it for advising during their first quarter of enrollment.

Prior to the 2025-2026 academic year, the Ancient Studies Minor was called the Classical Studies Minor. PREPARATION FOR THE MINOR Students are encouraged to take elementary courses in ancient Greek or Latin language to prepare for this minor. Students who unable to complete these courses prior to transfer should plan to enroll in GREE 1 – Elementary Ancient Greek or LATN 1 – Elementary Latin during their first quarter at UC Santa Cruz. These courses are offered only in the fall quarter. Students with prior knowledge of ancient Greek or Latin should consult with Ancient Studies Director to determine whether they have satisfied any elementary language course requirements. Visit the Ancient Studies website to learn more about this minor. UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Ancient Studies Minor Program Statement
